mnemonics.title: INSERT Syntax Pattern
mnemonics.content: INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (val1, val2) - Think: 'Into the table, put these values' - The order of columns must match values.

mnemonics.title: UPDATE Remember Always Needs WHERE
mnemonics.content: UPDATE without WHERE equals disaster! Always think: 'Which rows? Which rows?' before executing UPDATE.

mnemonics.title: DELETE Order of Operations
mnemonics.content: DELETE FROM table WHERE condition - Think: 'From where, delete what?' - FROM first, then WHERE clarifies what.

acronyms.usage: Category of SQL statements (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) that manipulate data in tables
Category (CREATE, DROP, ALTER) that defines table structures
Commands (COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVEPOINT) that manage transactions
Four basic database operations; INSERT=Create, SELECT=Read, UPDATE=Update, DELETE=Delete

visualTips.visualization: Think of INSERT as filling a form: Table name = form title, Column list = field names, VALUES = your填写 data
Imagine UPDATE as a spotlight: SET is the paint brush (what to change), WHERE is the flashlight (which rows to affect)
DELETE without WHERE is like using a nuke - it wipes everything! Always double-check the WHERE clause.

analogies.analogy: INSERT is like adding new students to a class, UPDATE is like changing a student's marks, DELETE is like removing a student from the roster.
COMMIT is like saving a document, ROLLBACK is like Ctrl+Z - it undoes your unsaved changes.
WHERE clause in UPDATE/DELETE is like the scope of a laser - narrow scope affects few, no scope affects everything!
